items.q: What attitude does Pop Art take toward consumer culture?
items.correct: Ambiguous - it both celebrates and critiques mass media imagery.
items.why: Students read Pop Art as either pure celebration or pure protest. Warhol's deadpan repetition of Marilyn or soup cans is deliberately AMBIGUOUS, leaving viewers to decide if it's homage or critique.

items.q: What is the first step of formal analysis?
items.correct: Description - noting what is actually present in the work.
items.why: Students jump to meaning immediately. Formal analysis follows a sequence: describe, analyse formal elements, interpret, evaluate. Description grounds interpretation in what's actually there.

items.q: What did Pollock mean by his drip paintings being 'in the painting'?
items.correct: He worked from all sides, making the act of painting central to the meaning.
items.misconception: He literally fell into wet paint.
items.why: Students take 'in the painting' literally. Pollock meant his BODILY ENGAGEMENT - working around and over the canvas on the floor - was integral to the work, not just the marks left behind.
The answer examiners want is: 1929. “1919” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.
The answer examiners want is: Polka dot. “Stripe” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: If Kusama returned to Japan in 1973 and has lived in hospital since 1977, how many years between return and hospital admission?
items.why: Exact answer: 4. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
